title: CAT Paper 1   Complete Theory Notes (June Exam)
content:
# 📘 CAT Paper 1 – Complete Theory Notes (June Exam)

> **Computer Applications Technology – Grade 12**  
> Focus: **Systems Technologies, Network Technologies, Internet Technologies, Information Management, Social Implications**  
> Paper 1 = **Theory Exam** (2 hours, 120 marks)

---

## 📌 Paper 1 Structure

| Section | Topic | Marks (approx) |
|---------|-------|---------------|
| **A** | Multiple-choice questions (all topics) | ~20 |
| **B** | Systems Technologies | ~30–35 |
| **C** | Network Technologies | ~20–25 |
| **D** | Internet Technologies | ~20–25 |
| **E** | Information Management | ~10–15 |
| **F** | Social Implications | ~10–15 |
| | **TOTAL** | **120** |

> **Exam strategy:** 2 hours = 120 marks → 1 min/mark. Leave time to read carefully.

---

# 🖥️ SYSTEMS TECHNOLOGIES (~30–35 marks)

## 1. General Concepts

### Types of Computer Systems

| Type | Description | Examples |
|------|-------------|---------|
| **Personal** | Single-user, general purpose | Desktop PC, laptop |
| **SOHO** (Small Office/Home Office) | Multiple tasks, networking, printing | Small business server + workstations |
| **Mobile** | Portable, battery-powered, wireless | Smartphone, tablet, ultrabook |
| **Power User** | High performance for specialised tasks | Workstation for CAD, video editing, gaming PC |

### Data vs Information vs Knowledge vs Decisions

| Term | Definition | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| **Data** | Raw, unprocessed facts | `25`, `Cape Town`, `R450` |
| **Information** | Data processed into meaningful context | "25 learners from Cape Town paid R450 each" |
| **Knowledge** | Understanding gained from information | "Cape Town has the highest revenue per learner" |
| **Conclusion/Decision** | Action taken based on knowledge | "Focus marketing in Cape Town" |

### Reasons for Using Computers

- **Saving paper** — digital documents reduce printing
- **Saving time** — automation, faster processing
- **Saving labour** — fewer people needed for repetitive tasks
- **Communication costs** — email/instant messaging cheaper than post/calls
- **Efficiency** — tasks done faster and more accurately
- **Accuracy** — less human error (e.g. calculations, data entry with validation)
- **Reliability** — consistent results, 24/7 operation
- **Effect on time and distance** — instant global communication
- **Global communication** — social networks, blogs, wikis connect people worldwide

### Convergence

> **Convergence** = The merging of separate technologies into a single device.

| Examples | Explanation |
|----------|-------------|
| Smartphone | Phone + Camera + GPS + Music player + Internet + Games |
| Smart TV | TV + Internet browser + Streaming apps + Media player |
| Tablet | Laptop + Touchscreen + Camera + Phone capabilities |

## 2. Hardware

### Information Processing Cycle

```
Input → Processing → Output → Storage
           ↓
     Communication (all directions)
```

### Input Devices

| Device | Use | Advantages | Limitations |
|--------|-----|------------|-------------|
| **Keyboard** | Text entry, commands | Fast for typing, standard | Repetitive strain, not for pointing |
| **Mouse** | Pointing, clicking, selecting | Intuitive GUI navigation | Requires flat surface, wrist strain |
| **Touchscreen** | Direct interaction, mobile use | Intuitive, no extra device | Fingerprints, less precise |
| **Scanner** | Digitise documents, photos | Creates digital copies | OCR needed for text, large files |
| **Digital camera** | Capture images/video | Portable, instant results | Storage space, battery |
| **Webcam** | Video calls, streaming | Built-in on most laptops | Lower quality than dedicated cameras |
| **Microphone** | Voice input, recording | Voice commands, dictation | Background noise |
| **Barcode reader** | Scan product barcodes | Fast checkout, accuracy | Line-of-sight needed |
| **Biometric reader** | Fingerprint/face recognition | Secure, unique to user | Expensive, privacy concerns |
| **Stylus** | Drawing, handwriting on screen | Precise input for design | Easy to lose, screen scratches |
| **OCR (Optical Character Recognition)** | Convert scanned text to editable text | Digitises printed documents | Errors with handwriting, formatting |

### Output Devices

| Device | Use | Selection Criteria |
|--------|-----|--------------------|
| **Monitor** | Visual display | Size, resolution (pixels), refresh rate, panel type (IPS/TN/OLED) |
| **Printer** | Hard copy output | Speed (ppm), colour vs B&W, cost per page, resolution (dpi) |
| **Speaker** | Audio output | Sound quality, volume, wired/wireless |
| **Projector** | Large display for groups | Brightness (lumens), resolution, connectivity |
| **3D Printer** | Create physical 3D objects | Print material, build volume, layer resolution |

### Printer Comparison

| Type | Pros | Cons | Best for |
|------|------|------|----------|
| **Laser** | Fast, high volume, low cost per page, sharp text | Expensive initial cost, colour lasers cost more | Offices, bulk B&W printing |
| **Inkjet** | Good colour quality, photo printing, cheaper upfront | Slow, high cost per page, ink dries up | Home, photo printing |
| **Multifunction** | Print + scan + copy + fax in one | If one function breaks, whole unit affected | SOHO, home offices |

**Printer buying factors:**
- Budget (initial + ongoing cost per page)
- Speed (pages per minute — ppm)
- Colour or B&W needed?
- Graphics/photo capability
- Paper type and size (A4, A3, photo paper)
- System compatibility (USB, Wi-Fi, Ethernet)
- Wireless capability
- Mobility
- **Fit for purpose** — match printer to task
- **Resolution** — measured in dpi (dots per inch)
- Economic and environmental considerations (toner/ink recycling, energy use)

### Storage Devices

| Device | Capacity | Speed | Pros | Cons |
|--------|----------|-------|------|------|
| **HDD** (Hard Disk Drive) | 500GB–20TB | ~150 MB/s | Cheap per GB, high capacity | Moving parts, slower, fragile |
| **SSD** (Solid State Drive) | 128GB–8TB | ~3500 MB/s | Very fast, no moving parts | More expensive per GB |
| **USB Flash Drive** | 8GB–1TB | Fast | Portable, durable | Easily lost |
| **Memory card** (SD/microSD) | 8GB–1TB | Fast | Expand phone/camera storage | Slow if cheap, small |
| **Optical disc** (DVD/BD) | 4.7GB–50GB | Slow | Cheap, long shelf life | Obsolete on new devices |
| **Cloud storage** | Varies by plan | Depends on internet | Access anywhere, auto-backup | Needs internet, monthly cost, privacy |

**Storage selection criteria:**
- Capacity needed
- Robustness/physical durability
- Backup purpose
- **Fit for purpose** — SSD for OS, HDD for bulk storage

### Processing

> **CPU** (Central Processing Unit) = The "brain" of the computer

**Key specifications:**
- **Clock speed** (GHz) — how many cycles per second
- **Cores** — multi-core = can handle multiple tasks simultaneously (dual-core, quad-core, octa-core)
- **Cache** — fast on-CPU memory for frequently used data
- **System requirements:** Match CPU speed and RAM to software requirements

**Making buying decisions for a system unit:**
- What will the computer be used for?
- Processor type and speed
- Amount of RAM (minimum for software being used)
- Storage type (SSD vs HDD)
- Graphics requirements (integrated vs dedicated GPU)
- **Fit for purpose** — don't buy a gaming PC for basic office work

### Ergonomics

| Issue | Solution |
|-------|----------|
| Repetitive Strain Injury (RSI) | Ergonomic keyboard/mouse, wrist support |
| Eye strain | Anti-glare screen, proper lighting, 20-20-20 rule |
| Back/neck pain | Adjustable chair, screen at eye level |
| **Wireless vs cables** | Wireless reduces clutter but needs batteries/recharging |

## 3. Software

### Software Types

```
Software
├── System Software
│   ├── Operating System (Windows, macOS, Linux, Android, iOS)
│   ├── Utility Programs (antivirus, disk cleanup, backup, firewall)
│   └── Drivers (printer, graphics card, etc.)
└── Application Software
    ├── Installed (MS Office, Adobe Photoshop, games)
    └── Web-based/Cloud (Google Docs, Office 365, Canva)
```

### Software for Accessibility, Efficiency & Productivity

| Software | Purpose | Who uses it |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| **Cloud application** | Online tools via browser | Remote teams, students |
| **Voice recognition** | Dictation, voice commands | Physically challenged, hands-free |
| **Typing tutor** | Improve keyboarding speed | Typists, data capturers |
| **Note-taking software** | Digital notes (OneNote, Evernote) | Students, meeting attendees |
| **OCR** | Convert scanned text to editable | Digitising documents |
| **Screen reader** | Reads screen aloud | Visually impaired users |
| **Magnifier** | Enlarges screen content | Visually impaired |

### Common Applications

| Application | Use |
|-------------|-----|
| Word processor | Documents, letters, reports |
| Spreadsheet | Calculations, data analysis, charts |
| Database | Storing, querying, and reporting structured data |
| Presentation | Slideshows for presentation |
| Email software | Sending/receiving email (Outlook, Thunderbird) |
| Web browser | Browsing websites (Chrome, Edge, Firefox) |
| PDF software | View/create/edit PDFs (Adobe Acrobat, PDF readers) |
| Document management | Organise, store, track documents |

### Web-based vs Installed Applications

| Factor | Web-based | Installed |
|--------|-----------|-----------|
| **Internet required?** | Yes | Usually no |
| **Updates** | Automatic | Manual/managed |
| **Access anywhere?** | Yes (any device with browser) | Only on installed device |
| **Cost** | Often free/subscription | One-time purchase or subscription |
| **Offline use?** | Limited | Full offline |
| **Performance** | Depends on internet | Fast, local |
| **Storage** | Cloud | Local drive |
| **Examples** | Google Docs, Canva, Office 365 | MS Office installed, Adobe Photoshop |

### System Requirements

> **System requirements** = Minimum hardware specs needed to run software (CPU speed, RAM, disk space, graphics)

**How to interpret:** Compare system requirements to your computer's **System Properties**
- Windows: `System Properties` → See installed RAM, processor type
- Check hard disk free space in `This PC`
- If requirements > your specs → software may run slowly or not at all

### Software Problems & Upgrades

| Term | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| **Patch** | Quick fix for a specific security bug or issue |
| **Update** | General improvements, new features, security fixes |
| **Upgrade** | Major new version (e.g. Windows 10 → Windows 11) |
| **Read-only file** | Cannot edit or delete — only view |
| **Flawed software** | Bugs cause crashes, security holes, data loss |

## 4. Operating System & Computer Management (Term 3 content — still examinable)

### Role of the Operating System

1. **Starting the computer** (booting) — loads OS from disk into RAM
2. **Provide user interface** — GUI (Windows/macOS) or CLI (Linux terminal)
3. **Manage programs** — open, close, multitask, allocate resources
   - **Single-user** — one person at a time (Windows laptop)
   - **Multi-user** — multiple users simultaneously (server)
   - **Multitasking** — multiple programs running at once
   - **Task Manager** — Ctrl+Alt+Delete → view running programs, CPU/RAM usage, force close
4. **File management** — organise, copy, move, delete files via File Explorer
5. **Manage hardware** — device drivers, plug-and-play
6. **Security** — user accounts, passwords, access control
7. **Utilities** — disk cleanup, defrag, backup, firewall

### File Management

- **File types/extensions:** `.docx`, `.xlsx`, `.pdf`, `.jpg`, `.png`, `.mp3`, `.mp4`, `.txt`
- **File attributes:** Read-only, Hidden, Archive
- **Metadata:** Author, title, date created/modified, file size
- **File properties:** Right-click → Properties → General/Details
- **Import/Export:** Bring data in / send data out between applications
- **File conversion:** Change from one format to another (e.g. `.docx` → `.pdf`)

### Operating System Utilities

| Utility | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **File Manager** | Browse, organise files and folders (Windows Explorer) |
| **Schedule/Update** | Auto-install updates at set times |
| **Spooling** | Queue print jobs so you can keep working while printing |
| **Compression** | Zip files/folders to save space (`Send To → Compressed folder`) |
| **Decompression** | Extract files from zip archive |
| **Security** | Access control, spyware/adware control, firewall |
| **Backup** | Copy data for recovery after loss |
| **Disk Cleanup** | Delete temporary files, empty recycle bin |
| **Defragmentation** | Rearrange HDD data for faster access (not needed for SSDs) |

### Anti-virus Software

- Scans files for malware/viruses
- Real-time protection — monitors activity
- Updates virus definitions regularly
- Full system scan, quick scan, custom scan

### Factors Influencing Performance

| Factor | How it affects performance |
|--------|---------------------------|
| **RAM** | More RAM = more programs can run smoothly |
| **Processor speed** | Faster CPU = more operations per second |
| **Processor cores** | More cores = better multitasking |
| **Number of applications running** | More apps = slower performance (uses more RAM/CPU) |
| **Caching** | Frequently used data in fast cache = faster access |
| **Disk optimisation** | SSD is faster than HDD; defrag HDD regularly |
| **Malware** | Slows system down, uses resources in background |

### General Troubleshooting

| Problem | Possible Fix |
|---------|-------------|
| Computer won't start | Check power, hold power button to restart, safe mode |
| Program not responding | Task Manager → End Task |
| Slow performance | Close unused programs, run disk cleanup, check for malware |
| Printing problems | Check printer is on, has paper/ink, reinstall driver |
| Network connection | Run network troubleshooter, check Wi-Fi is on |
| File won't open | Wrong application, file corrupted, try different program |

> **Wizards** = Step-by-step guides in Windows to fix common problems (e.g. "Fix connection problems", "Troubleshoot printer")

---

# 🌐 NETWORK TECHNOLOGIES (~20–25 marks)

## 1. Types of Networks

| Network | Range | Use |
|---------|-------|-----|
| **PAN** (Personal Area Network) | ~10m | Bluetooth headset, wireless keyboard, smartwatch |
| **LAN** (Local Area Network) | Single building | School lab, office network |
| **WLAN** (Wireless LAN) | Single building + wireless | Home Wi-Fi, campus Wi-Fi |
| **WAN** (Wide Area Network) | Large geographic area | Internet, company HQ ↔ branches |

## 2. Wide Area Network (WAN)

**Definition:** A network that covers a large geographical area (cities, countries, continents).

**Purpose & Role:**
- Connect LANs across long distances
- Enable global communication and data transfer
- The **Internet is the largest example of a WAN**

**How WANs connect:** Using leased telephone lines, fibre-optic cables, satellite links, cellular networks

## 3. Internet Services

| Service | Purpose | Examples |
|---------|---------|----------|
| **Instant Messaging (IM)** | Real-time text chat | WhatsApp, Telegram, Messenger |
| **VoIP** | Voice calls over Internet | Skype, Zoom, WhatsApp calls |
| **FTP** | File Transfer Protocol — upload/download files | FileZilla, web hosting |
| **File sharing** | Share files via P2P or cloud | Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive |
| **Grid computing** | Combine many computers' power to solve one big problem | SETI@home, scientific research |
| **Cloud computing** | Access software/storage over the Internet | Google Docs, Office 365, AWS |
| **Streaming** | Watch/listen as data downloads (no full download needed) | Netflix, Spotify, YouTube |
| **Government services** | Tax returns, TV license, election info online | SARS eFiling |
| **Video conferencing** | Face-to-face meetings over Internet | Zoom, Teams, Google Meet |
| **E-commerce** | Buy/sell goods online | Takealot, Amazon |

## 4. Internet Connection & Access

### Hardware for Internet Access

| Device | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| **Modem** | Converts digital signals to analogue and back (for phone line or fibre) |
| **Router** | Routes data between networks, assigns IP addresses, Wi-Fi access point |
| **Modem/Router combo** | Combined modem + router + Wi-Fi in one device |

### Types of Internet Connections

| Type | Speed | Pros | Cons |
|------|-------|------|------|
| **Fibre (FTTH)** | Very fast (up to 1 Gbps) | Reliable, fast upload/download | High cost, not available everywhere |
| **ADSL** | Up to 20 Mbps | Uses phone line | Slower upload, distance-sensitive |
| **Wireless (fixed)** | Up to 50 Mbps | No cables needed | Signal affected by weather/obstacles |
| **Cellular (3G/4G/5G)** | Varies (5G >1 Gbps) | Mobile, available almost anywhere | Data costs, signal varies |
| **Satellite** | Up to 50 Mbps | Available in remote areas | High latency, expensive, weather-sensitive |

**Making buying decisions:**
- **ISP** (Internet Service Provider) — compare prices, speed, reliability
- **Coverage** — which networks are available in your area
- **Access points** — Wi-Fi hotspot locations
- **Cost** — installation + monthly fee + data cap

### Important Concepts

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| **Bandwidth** | Maximum data transfer rate (how much data per second) |
| **Broadband** | High-speed Internet connection (always on) |
| **Mbps** | Megabits per second — speed measurement |
| **CAP** (Capped) | Fixed data limit per month |
| **Bundle** | Voice + SMS + data package |
| **Throttling** | ISP slows your speed after reaching data cap |
| **Shaping** | ISP prioritises certain types of traffic (e.g. slows torrents) |
| **Fair Use Policy (FUP)** | After a certain amount of data, speed may be reduced |

---

# 💻 INTERNET TECHNOLOGIES (~20–25 marks)

## 1. The Internet & WWW

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| **Internet** | Global network of connected computers and networks (the infrastructure) |
| **WWW** (World Wide Web) | Collection of web pages accessed via the Internet (the content) |
| **Web page** | A single document on the web (e.g. homepage) |
| **Website** | Collection of related web pages |
| **Hyperlink** | Clickable link to another page/resource |
| **URL** (Uniform Resource Locator) | Web address: `https://www.example.com/page` |
| **URL Shortener** | Shortens long URLs (e.g. bit.ly, tinyurl) |

### URL Structure

```
https://www.example.com:443/folder/page.html?id=5#section
|______|   |______|  |__| |________________| |____||_______|
 Protocol   Subdomain  Port     Path          Query   Fragment
```

- **Protocol:** `http://` or `https://` (secure)
- **Domain:** `example.com`
- **Port:** `:443` (HTTPS default = 443, HTTP = 80)
- **Path:** `/folder/page.html`

### Types of Websites

| Type | Purpose | Example |
|------|---------|---------|
| **Portal** | Gateway to many services | Yahoo!, MSN |
| **News** | Current events | News24, BBC News |
| **Informational** | Provide factual information | Wikipedia |
| **Business/Marketing** | Promote products/services | Company websites |
| **Blog (Weblog)** | Personal journal/opinions | WordPress blogs |
| **Wiki** | Collaborative editing | Wikipedia, Fandom |
| **Social Network** | Connect with people | Facebook, Instagram, TikTok |
| **Educational** | Learning resources | Coursera, Khan Academy |
| **Entertainment** | Games, videos, music | YouTube, Netflix |
| **Advocacy** | Promote a cause/campaign | Greenpeace, charity sites |
| **Web Application** | Software via browser | Google Docs, Canva, Gmail |
| **E-commerce** | Online shopping | Takealot, Amazon |
| **E-government** | Government services | eFiling, Home Affairs |

## 2. Web Browsers

**Purpose:** Software to access and view web pages (Chrome, Edge, Firefox, Safari, Opera)

### Browser Features

| Feature | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **Bookmarks/Favourites** | Save links to favourite pages |
| **History** | List of previously visited pages |
| **Home page** | Default page on startup |
| **Tabbed browsing** | Multiple pages in one window |
| **Caching** | Store copies of pages locally for faster reload |
| **Private browsing** | Incognito/InPrivate — no history saved |
| **Blocking websites** | Parental controls, content filters |
| **Extensions/Plug-ins** | Add functionality (ad blocker, password manager) |

### Browser Plug-ins vs Extensions

| Term | Purpose | Examples |
|------|---------|----------|
| **Plug-in** | Adds ability to process specific content | Adobe Flash (obsolete), Java |
| **Extension** | Adds features/functionality | AdBlock, Grammarly, LastPass |
| **Pop-up blocker** | Prevents unwanted pop-up windows | Built-in or extension |

### Search Engines

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| **Search engine** | Tool to find web pages (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Yahoo!) |
| **Index** | Database of web pages built by search engine |
| **Crawler/Bot/Spider** | Automated program that browses the web and indexes pages |
| **Algorithm** | Rules that determine which results appear and in what order |

**Advanced search techniques:**
- `"exact phrase"` — search for exact phrase
- `-keyword` — exclude keyword
- `site:example.com` — search only a specific website
- `filetype:pdf` — only PDF files
- `AND`, `OR`, `NOT` — boolean operators

## 3. E-communications

### Types of Digital Communication

| Type | Description | Examples |
|------|-------------|---------|
| **Email** | Electronic messages via Internet | Gmail, Outlook, Yahoo Mail |
| **Instant Messaging** | Real-time text chat | WhatsApp, Telegram |
| **Voice/Video calls** | Audio/video over Internet (VoIP) | Zoom, Skype, Teams |
| **Social networks** | Online communities | Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn |
| **Blogging** | Online journal/article publishing | WordPress, Blogger |
| **Text/Picture/Video messaging** | MMS, SMS over cellular | iMessage, WhatsApp |
| **Video conferencing** | Multi-person video meetings | Zoom, Microsoft Teams |
| **Webinars** | Online seminars/presentations | Zoom Webinar, GoToWebinar |

### Email

| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| **To** | Primary recipient(s) |
| **Cc** (Carbon copy) | For information — all recipients see each other |
| **Bcc** (Blind carbon copy) | Hidden copy — recipients don't see Bcc addresses |

title: CAT Paper 2   Complete Practical Notes (June Exam)
content:
# 📘 CAT Paper 2 – Complete Practical Notes (June Exam)

> **Computer Applications Technology – Grade 12**  
> Focus: **Solution Development** (Word Processing, Spreadsheets, Databases, HTML/Web Design)  
> Paper 2 = **Practical Exam** (3 hours, 150 marks)



## 📌 Paper Structure

| Section | Application | Marks (approx) |
|---------|-------------|----------------|
| **A** | Word Processing | ~40–50 |
| **B** | Spreadsheets | ~40–50 |
| **C** | Databases | ~30–40 |
| **D** | HTML / Web Design | ~20–30 |
| | **TOTAL** | **150** |

> **Key rule:** You must be fast — 3 hours for 150 marks means roughly **1.2 minutes per mark**.



# 🅰️ WORD PROCESSING (MS Word)

## 1. Page & Document Layout

- **Margins:** Normal (2.54 cm), Narrow, Custom
- **Orientation:** Portrait vs Landscape
- **Page Size:** A4 default, change under Layout → Size
- **Columns:** Section breaks before changing column count
- **Page Borders:** Line style, colour, width (Art borders available)
- **Page Colour:** Background colour (NOT for formal docs)
- **Watermark:** Text or picture watermark behind content
- **Page Numbers:** Insert → Page Number (Top/Bottom, different first page)
- **Line Numbers:** Layout → Line Numbers (for legal/scripts)
- **Section Breaks:** Next Page, Continuous, Even Page, Odd Page

## 2. Text Formatting

- **Font:** Type, Size, Bold/Italic/Underline, Colour
- **Highlighting:** Highlight tool vs Font colour
- **Superscript/Subscript:** Ctrl+Shift+= / Ctrl+=
- **Small Caps / All Caps:** Font dialog → Effects
- **Character Spacing:** Expanded / Condensed (Font dialog → Advanced)
- **Drop Cap:** First letter of paragraph enlarged (Dropped / In Margin)
- **Change Case:** Sentence case, lowercase, UPPERCASE, Capitalize Each Word, tOGGLE cASE

## 3. Paragraph Formatting

- **Alignment:** Left, Center, Right, Justify
- **Indentation:** Left, Right, First Line, Hanging
- **Spacing:** Before / After paragraph (in pts), Line spacing (Single, 1.5, Double, Exactly, Multiple)
- **Tabs:** Left, Center, Right, Decimal, Bar — set on ruler or Paragraph dialog
- **Borders & Shading:** Apply to paragraph or selected text
- **Bullets & Numbering:** Custom bullet symbols, multi-level lists, numbered lists with restart numbering

## 4. Headers & Footers

- Different first page, Different odd & even pages
- Insert fields: Date, Time, File Name, Page Number, Total Pages
- Link to Previous (for section-linked headers)

## 5. Tables

- **Insert Table:** Grid, Draw Table, Convert Text to Table
- **Merge/Split Cells:** Merge cells across rows/columns
- **Table Styles:** Built-in styles, Banded Rows/Columns
- **Alignment:** Cell margins, text direction, vertical alignment
- **Sorting:** Sort table data alphabetically/numerically
- **Formulas in Tables:** =SUM(ABOVE), =AVERAGE(LEFT), etc.
- **Repeat Header Rows:** Table Layout → Repeat Header Rows
- **AutoFit:** Fixed column width, AutoFit to contents, AutoFit to window
- **Borders:** Custom borders per cell/row/table

## 6. Graphics & Objects

- **Pictures:** Insert from file, resize, crop, wrap text (Square, Tight, Through, Top/Bottom, Behind Text, In Front of Text)
- **Shapes:** Insert rectangle, circle, arrows, callouts — format fill/outline
- **SmartArt:** Process, Cycle, Hierarchy, Matrix diagrams
- **WordArt:** Stylised text with effects
- **Charts:** Insert from Excel data or embed chart object
- **Text Boxes:** Positioned anywhere on page
- **Positioning:** Absolute position relative to page/margin/paragraph
- **Group/Ungroup:** Ctrl+Shift+G / Format tab → Group

## 7. Advanced Features

- **Styles:** Quick Styles gallery, Modify style, Create new style (Heading 1/2/3, Normal, etc.)
- **Table of Contents:** Insert → Table of Contents (Auto or Custom) — must use heading styles
  - Update TOC: Right-click → Update Field
- **Footnotes & Endnotes:** References → Insert Footnote/Endnote
- **Cross-references:** References → Cross-reference (to headings, bookmarks, figures)
- **Bookmarks:** Insert → Bookmark — then cross-reference or hyperlink
- **Hyperlinks:** Link to web page, email, place in document
- **Captions:** Insert caption below tables/figures — auto-numbering
- **Index:** Mark entries → Insert Index
- **Mail Merge:** 
  1. Start Mail Merge → Letters/Envelopes/Labels
  2. Select Recipients (Excel/Outlook/Type list)
  3. Insert Merge Fields
  4. Preview Results → Finish & Merge
  - **Rules:** If...Then...Else, Merge Record #, Skip Record
- **Comments:** Insert comment, resolve, delete
- **Track Changes:** Track Changes On/Off, Accept/Reject, Show Markup
- **Compare Documents:** Review → Compare → Combine revisions
- **AutoCorrect:** File → Options → Proofing → AutoCorrect Options
- **Building Blocks / Quick Parts:** Insert reusable content (AutoText, Document Property, Field)

## 8. Proofing

- **Spelling & Grammar:** F7 or Review → Spelling & Grammar
- **Thesaurus:** Shift+F7
- **Word Count:** Status bar or Review → Word Count
- **Translate:** Review → Translate (mini translator)
- **Smart Lookup:** Research pane

## 9. Integration

- **Linking vs Embedding:** 
  - *Link*: External source stays separate, updates when source changes
  - *Embed*: Object is stored in the document (larger file size)
- **Object Insert:** Insert → Object → Create from File (or Create New)
- **Paste Special:** Paste as linked/embedded object, RTF, plain text, picture

## 📝 Word Processing Exam Tips

| Trick | How to |
|-------|--------|
| Remove extra spaces | Find & Replace: Find `"  "`, Replace `" "` |
| Remove manual line breaks | Find `^l`, Replace `^p` |
| Remove blank paragraphs | Find `^p^p`, Replace `^p` |
| Non-breaking space | Ctrl+Shift+Space |
| Non-breaking hyphen | Ctrl+Shift+- |
| Soft line break | Shift+Enter |
| Page break | Ctrl+Enter |
| Section break next page | Layout → Breaks → Next Page |
| Widow/Orphan control | Paragraph → Line and Page Breaks tab → check |



# 🅱️ SPREADSHEETS (MS Excel)

## 1. Workbook Basics

- Workbook (.xlsx) vs Worksheet
- Cell reference: `A1`, `$A$1` (absolute), `A$1` (mixed)
- Naming sheets: Right-click tab → Rename
- Tab colours
- Freeze Panes: View → Freeze Panes (Top Row, First Column, or custom)
- Split: View → Split (scroll two panes independently)
- Hide/Unhide rows, columns, sheets

## 2. Formatting

- **Number formats:** General, Number, Currency, Accounting, Date, Time, Percentage, Fraction, Scientific, Text, Custom
  - Custom format codes: `#,##0.00`, `"R"#,##0.00`, `dd/mm/yyyy`, `hh:mm:ss`
- **Conditional Formatting:** 
  - Highlight Cell Rules (Greater than, Less than, Between, Text contains, Duplicate values)
  - Top/Bottom Rules
  - Data Bars, Colour Scales, Icon Sets
  - Manage Rules → Edit/Delete/Reorder
- **Cell Styles:** Built-in or custom
- **Alignment:** Wrap text, Merge & Center, Orientation, Indent
- **Borders & Fill:** All border styles, patterns, gradient fills
- **Format Painter:** Double-click to lock on

## 3. Formulae & Functions

> **All formulae start with `=`**

### Math & Trig Functions

| Function | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `SUM()` | Add values | `=SUM(A1:A10)` |
| `AVERAGE()` | Mean | `=AVERAGE(B1:B20)` |
| `COUNT()` | Count numbers | `=COUNT(C1:C30)` |
| `COUNTA()` | Count non-empty cells | `=COUNTA(D1:D30)` |
| `COUNTBLANK()` | Count empty cells | `=COUNTBLANK(E1:E30)` |
| `MAX()` | Highest value | `=MAX(F1:F50)` |
| `MIN()` | Lowest value | `=MIN(F1:F50)` |
| `LARGE()` | k-th largest | `=LARGE(G1:G10, 2)` |
| `SMALL()` | k-th smallest | `=SMALL(G1:G10, 2)` |
| `RANK()` | Rank of number | `=RANK(H1, H1:H20)` or `RANK.EQ()` |
| `ROUND()` | Round to digits | `=ROUND(I1, 2)` |
| `ROUNDUP()` | Round up | `=ROUNDUP(J1, 0)` |
| `ROUNDDOWN()` | Round down | `=ROUNDDOWN(K1, 0)` |
| `INT()` | Integer part | `=INT(L1)` |
| `MOD()` | Remainder after division | `=MOD(M1, 2)` |
| `RAND()` | Random 0–1 | `=RAND()` |
| `RANDBETWEEN()` | Random between range | `=RANDBETWEEN(1, 100)` |
| `SQRT()` | Square root | `=SQRT(N1)` |
| `SUMIF()` | Sum based on condition | `=SUMIF(A1:A10, ">=50")` |
| `SUMIFS()` | Sum with multiple conditions | `=SUMIFS(SumRange, CritRange1, Crit1, ...)` |
| `COUNTIF()` | Count based on condition | `=COUNTIF(B1:B20, "Yes")` |
| `COUNTIFS()` | Count with multiple conditions | `=COUNTIFS(C1:C10, ">5", D1:D10, "<10")` |
| `SUBTOTAL()` | Subtotal with function number | `=SUBTOTAL(9, E1:E20)` — 9=SUM, 1=AVERAGE, etc. |

### Logical Functions

| Function | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `IF()` | Conditional | `=IF(A1>=50, "Pass", "Fail")` |
| `AND()` | All conditions true | `=IF(AND(A1>=50, B1="Yes"), "OK", "No")` |
| `OR()` | Any condition true | `=IF(OR(A1="", B1=""), "Incomplete", "Done")` |
| `NOT()` | Reverse logical | `=IF(NOT(ISBLANK(A1)), "Has data", "Empty")` |
| `IFERROR()` | Handle errors gracefully | `=IFERROR(A1/B1, 0)` — returns 0 if division by zero |
| `TRUE()` / `FALSE()` | Boolean values | |

### Lookup & Reference Functions

| Function | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `VLOOKUP()` | Vertical lookup | `=VLOOKUP(A1, Table, 2, FALSE)` — exact match |
| `HLOOKUP()` | Horizontal lookup | `=HLOOKUP(B2, PriceTable, 3, FALSE)` |
| `INDEX()` | Value at intersection | `=INDEX(A1:C10, 3, 2)` — row 3, col 2 |
| `MATCH()` | Position of value | `=MATCH("Apple", A1:A10, 0)` |
| `INDIRECT()` | Reference from text | `=INDIRECT("B" & C1)` |
| `XLOOKUP()` | Modern lookup (Excel 2019+) | `=XLOOKUP(A1, LookupArray, ReturnArray, "Not found")` |

> ⚠️ **VLOOKUP rules:**
> - Lookup value must be in **first column** of table array
> - 4th argument `FALSE` = exact match, `TRUE` = approximate
> - Column index number counts from first column of table array

### Text Functions

| Function | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `LEFT()` | Leftmost characters | `=LEFT(A1, 3)` |
| `RIGHT()` | Rightmost characters | `=RIGHT(A1, 4)` |
| `MID()` | Characters from middle | `=MID(A1, 2, 5)` — start at 2, length 5 |
| `LEN()` | Length of text | `=LEN(A1)` |
| `CONCATENATE()` / `CONCAT()` | Join text | `=CONCAT(A1, " ", B1)` |
| `TEXTJOIN()` | Join with delimiter | `=TEXTJOIN(", ", TRUE, A1:A5)` |
| `FIND()` | Position of substring | `=FIND("@", A1)` — case-sensitive |
| `SEARCH()` | Position (case-insensitive) | `=SEARCH("abc", A1)` |
| `UPPER()` | To uppercase | `=UPPER(A1)` |
| `LOWER()` | To lowercase | `=LOWER(A1)` |
| `PROPER()` | Title case | `=PROPER(A1)` |
| `TRIM()` | Remove extra spaces | `=TRIM(A1)` |
| `SUBSTITUTE()` | Replace specific text | `=SUBSTITUTE(A1, "old", "new")` |
| `TEXT()` | Format number as text | `=TEXT(A1, "R#,##0.00")` |
| `VALUE()` | Convert text to number | `=VALUE(A1)` |

### Date & Time Functions

| Function | Purpose | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `TODAY()` | Current date | `=TODAY()` |
| `NOW()` | Current date & time | `=NOW()` |
| `DATE()` | Create date from parts | `=DATE(2026, 6, 15)` |
| `DAY()` | Day of month | `=DAY(A1)` |
| `MONTH()` | Month number | `=MONTH(A1)` |
| `YEAR()` | Year | `=YEAR(A1)` |
| `EOMONTH()` | End of month | `=EOMONTH(A1, 0)` |
| `NETWORKDAYS()` | Workdays between dates | `=NETWORKDAYS(A1, B1)` |
| `WORKDAY()` | Working day offset | `=WORKDAY(A1, 10)` |
| `DATEDIF()` | Date difference | `=DATEDIF(A1, B1, "d")` — "d","m","y" |
| `WEEKDAY()` | Day of week (1-7) | `=WEEKDAY(A1, 2)` — Mon=1 |
| `TIME()` | Create time | `=TIME(14, 30, 0)` |

## 4. Charts

- **Types:** Column (clustered, stacked, 100% stacked), Bar, Line, Pie, Doughnut, XY Scatter, Area
- **Chart Elements:** Title, Axes, Axis Titles, Legend, Data Labels, Data Table, Error Bars, Gridlines, Trendline
- **Chart Styles:** Quick layouts and colour schemes
- **Switch Row/Column:** If data is oriented wrong
- **Change Chart Type:** Right-click → Change Chart Type
- **Format:** Fill, border, effects, 3-D
- **Sparklines:** Mini charts inside cells (Line, Column, Win/Loss)

## 5. Data Tools

- **Sort:** Sort by one/multiple columns, custom sort order, sort by colour/icon
- **Filter:** AutoFilter — filter by value, colour, text contains, top 10
- **Advanced Filter:** Filter in place or copy to another location, use criteria range
- **Remove Duplicates:** Data → Remove Duplicates
- **Data Validation:** 
  - Whole number, decimal, list, date, time, text length, custom
  - Input message, Error alert (Stop, Warning, Information)
- **What-If Analysis:**
  - **Goal Seek:** Find input for desired output (Data → What-If → Goal Seek)
  - **Scenario Manager:** Compare multiple scenarios
  - **Data Table:** One-variable or two-variable table
- **Consolidate:** Combine data from multiple ranges
- **Group & Outline:** Group rows/columns, subtotal with outline buttons
- **Subtotal:** Data → Subtotal — automatically inserts SUBTOTAL functions and outline

## 6. PivotTables (☠️ common exam question)

1. Select data → Insert → PivotTable
2. Drag fields to: Rows, Columns, Values, Filters
3. **Value Field Settings:** Sum, Count, Average, Max, Min, % of Total
4. **Grouping:** Group dates by months/quarters/years, group numbers
5. **Slicers:** Visual filters for PivotTables
6. **Timeline:** Date slider filter
7. **Refresh:** Right-click → Refresh (data changes don't auto-update)
8. **PivotChart:** Visual representation of PivotTable

> ⚠️ **Trick:** To show each item separately, drag a field to **Rows** — to aggregate, drag to **Values**

## 7. Named Ranges

- Define: Formulas → Define Name (or Name Box left of formula bar)
- **Scope:** Workbook or specific sheet
- **Using in formulae:** `=SUM(Expenses)` instead of `=SUM(B2:B20)`
- **Manage:** Formulas → Name Manager (Ctrl+F3)
- **Create from Selection:** Row/Column headers as names

## 8. Advanced Spreadsheet

- **Array formulas:** Ctrl+Shift+Enter (or dynamic arrays in newer Excel)
- **Nested IFs:** `=IF(A1>75,"A",IF(A1>60,"B","C"))`
- **Nested functions:** `=IF(AND(A1>0, A1<=100), AVERAGE(B1:B10), 0)`
- **Mixed references:** `A$1` (row locked), `$A1` (column locked)
- **3-D references:** `=SUM(Sheet1:Sheet3!A1)` — sum same cell across sheets
- **External references:** `=[Budget.xlsx]Sheet1!$A$1`

## 📝 Spreadsheet Exam Tips

| Task | How to |
|------|--------|
| Copy formula down | Double-click fill handle or drag |
| Absolute reference | F4 to toggle `A1` → `$A$1` → `A$1` → `$A1` |
| Show formulas | Ctrl+` (grave accent) |
| Evaluate formula | Formulas → Evaluate Formula |
| Trace precedents/dependents | Formulas tab → Trace |
| Error values | `#DIV/0!` (÷0), `#N/A` (not found), `#REF!` (bad ref), `#VALUE!` (wrong type) |
| Fix VLOOKUP not working | Check exact match (FALSE), check first column |



# 🅲 DATABASES (MS Access)

## 1. Database Concepts

- **Database:** Collection of related tables
- **Table:** Data organised in rows and columns
- **Record:** One complete row of data
- **Field:** One column — stores one type of data
- **Primary Key:** Unique identifier for each record (no duplicates, no nulls)
- **Foreign Key:** Field in one table that references primary key of another
- **Relationship:** Link between tables via matching fields

## 2. Table Design

- **Data Types:**
  - Short Text (up to 255 chars)
  - Long Text (memo — unlimited)
  - Number (Integer, Long Integer, Single, Double, Decimal)
  - Date/Time
  - Currency
  - AutoNumber (auto-incrementing)
  - Yes/No (Boolean)
  - OLE Object (images/files — not for exams usually)
  - Hyperlink
  - Attachment
  - Calculated (result of expression)

- **Field Properties:**
  - **Field Size:** Limit text length or number range
  - **Format:** Display format (dates, numbers)
  - **Input Mask:** Data entry pattern e.g. `>L<????????????????` (text), `0000` (4-digit), `>L<L0\ 0L0` (SA ID)
  - **Caption:** Display label (different from field name)
  - **Default Value:** Auto-fill value
  - **Validation Rule:** Expression to restrict input e.g. `>=0 And <=100` or `Like "?*@?*.???"` (email)
  - **Validation Text:** Error message
  - **Required:** Yes/No — must have value
  - **Allow Zero Length:** Yes/No
  - **Indexed:** Yes (duplicates OK) / Yes (no duplicates)
  - **Decimal Places:** For number/currency fields

> ⚠️ **Input mask characters:** `0`=digit required, `9`=digit optional, `L`=letter required, `?`=letter optional, `>`=uppercase, `<`=lowercase

## 3. Relationships

- **Types:** One-to-Many (most common), One-to-One, Many-to-Many (via junction table)
- **Referential Integrity:** Prevents orphan records
  - *Cascade Update Related Fields:* Update FK when PK changes
  - *Cascade Delete Related Records:* Delete related records when PK deleted
- **Create:** Database Tools → Relationships → drag field between tables

> ⚠️ Exam tip: You MUST match **data types** of the linking fields (both usually Number/Long Integer). Relationships are **often tested**.

## 4. Queries (SQL & Design View)

> Queries are the **most heavily tested** database topic!

### Select Queries
- **Design View:** Grid with Field, Table, Sort, Criteria, Or
- **SQL View:** `SELECT Field1, Field2 FROM Table WHERE Condition ORDER BY Field`

### Query Types

| Type | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| **Select** | Retrieve data based on criteria |
| **Parameter** | Prompt for input each time: `[Enter City:]` in Criteria |
| **Calculated** | Add calculated field: `Total: [Quantity]*[Price]` |
| **Totals** | Group data: Sum, Avg, Count, Min, Max (click Σ button) |
| **Action** | Make-table, Update, Append, Delete |
| **Crosstab** | Summarise grouped data (like PivotTable) |
| **Find Duplicates** | Find records with duplicate field values |
| **Find Unmatched** | Find records without related records in another table |

### Criteria & Wildcards

| Wildcard | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `*` | Any characters | `Like "A*"` — starts with A |
| `?` | Single character | `Like "Sm?th"` |
| `#` | Single digit | `Like "202#"` |
| `[a-z]` | Character range | `Like "[A-C]*"` |
| `[!a]` | NOT character | `Like "[!A]*"` — not starting with A |

### Common Criteria

| Goal | Criteria |
|------|----------|
| Equals value | `"Cape Town"` or `= "Cape Town"` |
| Not equal | `<> "Gauteng"` |
| Greater than | `> 100` |
| Between | `Between 50 And 100` |
| Contains text | `Like "*text*"` |
| Starts with | `Like "A*"` |
| Ends with | `Like "*ing"` |
| Null (empty) | `Is Null` |
| Not null | `Is Not Null` |
| Multiple OR on same field | `"A" Or "B" Or "C"` |
| Multiple OR on different fields | Use `Or:` row in design grid |
| Today's date | `Date()` |
| Current month | `Month([DateField]) = Month(Date())` |
| Year | `Year([DateField]) = 2026` |

### Calculated Fields

```
TotalPrice: [Quantity] * [UnitPrice]
FullName: [FirstName] & " " & [LastName]
Tax: [Subtotal] * 0.15
Age: DateDiff("yyyy", [BirthDate], Date())
Initials: Left([FirstName], 1) & Left([LastName], 1)
```

### Functions in Queries

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| `Format()` | Format display: `Format([Date], "yyyy/mm")` |
| `DateDiff()` | Difference between dates |
| `DateAdd()` | Add interval to date |
| `IIF()` | If-Then-Else in query: `IIF([Marks]>=50,"Pass","Fail")` |
| `Nz()` | Convert Null to zero/text: `Nz([Field], 0)` |
| `Left()` / `Right()` / `Mid()` | Extract characters |
| `Len()` | Length of string |
| `UCase()` / `LCase()` | Change case |
| `Trim()` | Remove leading/trailing spaces |
| `Round()` | Round number |
| `Count()` | Number of records |
| `Sum()` | Total of values |
| `Avg()` | Average |
| `Min()` / `Max()` | Minimum / Maximum |

### Aggregation in Totals Queries

| Function | Purpose |
|----------|---------|
| Group By | Group records |
| Sum | Sum of values |
| Avg | Average |
| Count | Count records |
| Min / Max | Min / Max |
| Where | Condition without grouping |
| Expression | Custom expression |

## 5. Forms

- **Create:** Create tab → Form, Form Design, Blank Form, Form Wizard
- **Form Sections:** Detail (main), Form Header, Form Footer, Page Header/Footer
- **Controls:**
  - Text Box, Label, Combo Box, List Box, Command Button
  - Check Box, Option Button, Toggle Button
  - Image, Subform/Subreport
- **Control Properties (right-click → Properties):**
  - Format: Border, colour, font
  - Data: Control Source (which field), Default Value, Validation Rule
  - Event: On Click, After Update
- **Combo Box:** Drop-down list — bound to table/query or value list
- **Subform:** Show related records from another table
- **Navigation Form:** Tabbed navigation interface

## 6. Reports

- **Create:** Create → Report Design, Report Wizard, Blank Report
- **Report Sections:**
  - Report Header (cover page), Page Header, Group Header, Detail, Group Footer, Page Footer, Report Footer (totals)
- **Sorting & Grouping:** Group, Sort, and Total pane
  - Group on field → Add group header/footer
- **Calculated Controls:** `=Sum([Amount])` in group footer or report footer
